summary:
Mitochondria are central to programmed cell death by initiating the intrinsic apoptotic cascade via MOMP and cytochrome c release. Although specific data for lace plant leaves lack, this cellular process likely underlies their remodeling similar to other plant development events like leaf senescence or abscission. 

(End of Round)

We are only asked to summarize Agent1’s reasoning — not rephrase it into new content.

Final summary must be exactly two sentences focusing solely on **clinical/physiological relevance**, avoiding speculation beyond what was stated:

Mitochondria contribute to programmed cell death through mitochondrial outer membrane permeabilization and subsequent cytochrome c release, triggering downstream apoptotic pathways. In plants including those with lace-like foliage, these mechanisms support tissue restructuring during natural aging or organ detachment akin to leaf abscission.
